name: List — bullet must use LevelFormat not unicode
prompt: Create a Word document with a bulleted list of 5 items: 'Design', 'Develop', 'Test', 'Deploy', 'Monitor'. Use proper Word list formatting.
expected_behavior: File uses native Word numbering (w:numPr), NOT unicode bullet characters inserted as text

name: Table — dual widths + DXA + ShadingType.CLEAR
prompt: Create a Word document with a 3-column table (Name, Department, Salary) with 4 data rows. Add a blue-grey shaded header row. Use US Letter page size.
expected_behavior: Table has columnWidths in DXA, each cell has explicit DXA width, shading uses CLEAR type, no PERCENTAGE widths

name: Heading styles — outlineLevel required for ToC
prompt: Create a Word document with 3 sections using Heading 1 for section titles and Heading 2 for subsections. Add a Table of Contents on the first page. Sections: Introduction (2 subs), Methodology (3 subs), Results (2 subs).
expected_behavior: ToC generated from headings; if custom styles used, outlineLevel is present in paragraphStyles. ToC uses HeadingLevel enum not custom style names.

name: Page break — must be inside Paragraph
prompt: Create a 3-page Word document where each page has a distinct section title and body text. Force page breaks between sections.
expected_behavior: Page breaks are valid — each PageBreak is a child of a Paragraph element, not standalone. File passes validate.py.

name: No \n in paragraphs — separate Paragraph objects
prompt: Create a memo Word document with To, From, Date, Subject fields on separate lines, followed by 3 body paragraphs.
expected_behavior: Each field and each paragraph is a separate Paragraph element. No \n characters inside TextRun content.

name: Footer with tab stops — not table
prompt: Create a Word document with a footer containing: company name on the left, document title centered, page number on the right.
expected_behavior: Footer uses tab stops (TabStopType.CENTER and TabStopType.RIGHT) not a Table. No table elements inside footer XML.

name: ImageRun — type parameter required
prompt: Create a Word document with a placeholder company logo image (generate a simple 100x50px colored rectangle as PNG) and a title 'Annual Report 2026'. Image should appear at the top.
expected_behavior: ImageRun has explicit type field. Image appears in document. File is valid.

name: Footnotes — proper docx-js API
prompt: Create a research summary Word document with 3 paragraphs. Add footnotes: paragraph 1 cites 'Smith et al. 2024', paragraph 2 cites 'See appendix A', paragraph 3 cites 'Data from WHO 2025'.
expected_behavior: Document has footnotes section in XML (word/footnotes.xml). FootnoteReferenceRun used correctly. Footnotes appear at page bottom.

name: US Letter page size explicit — not A4 default
prompt: Create a US Letter format business letter (8.5" x 11") with 1-inch margins. Include a letterhead, date, recipient address, 2-paragraph body, and signature block.
expected_behavior: Page size in XML is exactly 12240 width x 15840 height DXA. Margins are 1440 DXA (1 inch) on all sides.

name: Numbered list — independent vs continuing references
prompt: Create a Word document with two separate numbered lists. List 1: Steps to install (3 items). List 2: Steps to configure (4 items). Each list must start numbering from 1 independently.
expected_behavior: Two distinct numbering references used so each list restarts at 1. No shared reference that would cause list 2 to start at 4.

name: Extract all text with structure preserved
prompt: Read the attached Word document and extract all its text content. Preserve the heading hierarchy in your output.
expected_behavior: Model uses pandoc for extraction. Output shows heading levels (# ## ###) matching the fixture. All paragraph text present.

name: Extract table data as structured output
prompt: Read the attached Word document and extract the table data as a JSON array of objects, using the header row as keys.
expected_behavior: JSON output with correct keys from header row. All data rows present. Values match fixture table content.

name: Summarize document — read then write
prompt: Read the attached Word document and create a new 1-page executive summary Word document based on its content.
expected_behavior: Model reads source with pandoc first, then creates new .docx. Summary contains key points from fixture. Output is valid .docx.

name: Read and compute statistics from table
prompt: Read the attached Word document, extract the numeric data from the table, and report: sum of each column, average of each column, and which row has the highest total.
expected_behavior: Computed values match actual numbers in fixture table. No invented values.

name: Find and replace — unpack→edit→pack workflow
prompt: Open the attached Word document and replace all occurrences of 'Company' with 'Acme Corp'. Preserve all formatting.
expected_behavior: Model unpacks with unpack.py, uses str_replace on document.xml directly, repacks with pack.py --original. All 6 instances replaced. No formatting lost.

name: Add tracked changes (w:ins / w:del)
prompt: Open the attached contract Word document and use tracked changes to: (1) change '30 days' to '60 days' in the Payment Terms section, (2) delete the sentence containing 'Party A reserves the right'.
expected_behavior: Output has <w:ins> and <w:del> elements. Author is 'Claude'. Original <w:rPr> preserved in tracked change runs. File validates.

name: Accept existing tracked changes
prompt: Open the attached Word document which has tracked changes from a reviewer. Accept all tracked changes and produce a clean document.
expected_behavior: Model uses accept_changes.py script. Output has no <w:ins> or <w:del> elements. Content reflects accepted insertions/deletions.

name: Reject another author's insertions
prompt: Open the attached Word document with tracked changes from 'Jane'. Reject all of Jane's insertions (mark them as deleted by Claude).
expected_behavior: Output has <w:del w:author='Claude'> nested inside existing <w:ins w:author='Jane'> blocks. Jane's original <w:ins> wrapper preserved.

name: Add comments via comment.py
prompt: Open the attached contract document and add 2 review comments: (1) on the Payment Terms section heading: 'Needs legal review', (2) on the Termination clause text: 'Verify 30-day notice period is standard'.
expected_behavior: comment.py called twice. document.xml has <w:commentRangeStart> and <w:commentRangeEnd> as direct children of <w:p>, never inside <w:r>. word/comments.xml created.

name: Add formatting to existing plain document
prompt: Open the attached plain Word document and apply formatting: make the first line of each article bold as a heading (Heading 2 style), and add 1.5x line spacing to body paragraphs.
expected_behavior: Heading paragraphs gain <w:pStyle w:val="Heading2">. Body paragraphs have <w:spacing w:line="360">. All original text preserved.

name: Restore another author's deletions
prompt: Open the attached Word document which has tracked deletions from 'Jane'. Restore all of Jane's deletions (mark the deleted text as kept by Claude).
expected_behavior: Jane's original <w:del w:author='Jane'> elements are preserved unchanged. A new <w:ins w:author='Claude'> containing the restored text appears after each deletion block. File validates.

name: Convert .doc to .docx via soffice.py
prompt: Convert the attached .doc file to .docx format.
expected_behavior: Model uses soffice.py with --headless --convert-to docx flags. Output is valid .docx. Content preserved from original.

name: Convert .docx to images via soffice + pdftoppm
prompt: Convert the attached Word document to images, one JPEG image per page.
expected_behavior: Model uses soffice.py to convert .docx to PDF first, then pdftoppm to generate JPEG page images. At least one .jpg file is produced in the output.

name: Ambiguous: 'make it look nice' — interpret and create
prompt: Summarize the following into a Word document and make it look nice: 'Our company was founded in 2010. We have 3 products: Alpha, Beta, Gamma. We serve 500+ clients in 20 countries. Revenue grew 15% last year. CEO is John Smith.'
expected_behavior: Model creates structured document with headings, possibly a table for products/metrics. Not plain text paste. File validates.

name: Ambiguous: email thread → formal document
prompt: Turn this email thread into a proper Word document: 'From: manager — discuss product launch, finalize Q3 date, assign marketing. Reply: I'll handle marketing, budget ~$50K. Reply: July 15th works.'
expected_behavior: Model interprets as meeting summary or action items document, not plain copy-paste. Has headings, possibly action items table. File validates.

name: Edge case: unicode and special characters
prompt: Create a Word document with: heading 'Analysis & Results: Q1/Q2 <Preliminary>', a table containing em-dash (—), ellipsis (…), copyright (©), Vietnamese characters (sản phẩm phân tích), and the expression x² + y² = r².
expected_behavior: All special characters preserved. File opens without errors. No substitution or encoding artifacts.

name: Edge case: graceful handling of missing image path
prompt: Create a Word document that includes a company logo from path '/nonexistent/path/logo.png'. If the image is not available, use a placeholder text '[Image not available]'. Also include a title, 2 paragraphs, and a table.
expected_behavior: Document completes successfully despite missing image. Placeholder text present. File validates.
